content.problemStatement:
Suppose f(X,Y,Z)f(X, Y, Z)f(X,Y,Z) denotes the number of arrays AAA of length XXX such that:


1‚Č§Ai‚Č§Y1 \leq A_i \leq Y1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§Y, for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§X)(1 \leq i \leq X)(1‚Č§i‚Č§X);
There are **at most** ZZZ values of iii (2‚Č§i‚Č§X)(2 \leq i \leq X)(2‚Č§i‚Č§X) such that Ai=Ai‚ąí1A_i=A_{i-1}Ai‚Äč=Ai‚ąí1‚Äč. In other words, for at most ZZZ indices, the element at a given index is equal to the previous element.
You are given an integer MMM and QQQ queries. The ithi^{th}ith query is of the form:


NiN_iNi‚Äč KiK_iKi‚Äč: Given integers NiN_iNi‚Äč and KiK_iKi‚Äč, find the value f(Ni,M,Ki)f(N_i,M,K_i)f(Ni‚Äč,M,Ki‚Äč).
Since output might be too large, print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer MMM.
The second line of input contains an integer QQQ, the number of queries.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two integers NiN_iNi‚Äč and KiK_iKi‚Äč.


Output Format
Print QQQ lines, where, in the ithi^{th}ith line, print a single integer f(Ni,M,Ki)f(N_i,M,K_i)f(Ni‚Äč,M,Ki‚Äč), the answer for ithi^{th}ith query modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
